response.extracted_text: The Human Cost: System Waste & Burnout Administrative waste does not merely consume dollars — it consumes physicians. Excessive documentation, prior authorizations, and bureaucratic overhead translate directly into emotional exhaustion, depersonalization, and reduced efficacy: the clinical triad of burnout. Emotional Exhaustion Depleted by relentless non-clinical demands Depersonalization Disconnection from patients as a protective response Reduced Efficacy Loss of meaning and confidence in clinical contribution
response.layout_description: The slide is split vertically with a full-height photo of an exhausted physician on the left and a white content area on the right. The right side contains a large title, a paragraph, two side-by-side callout cards, and one wide callout card below them.
response.slide_title: The Human Cost: System Waste & Burnout
text_blocks: The Human Cost: System Waste & Burnout

Administrative waste does not merely consume dollars — it consumes physicians. Excessive documentation, prior authorizations, and bureaucratic overhead translate directly into emotional exhaustion, depersonalization, and reduced efficacy: the clinical triad of burnout.
Emotional Exhaustion Depleted by relentless non-clinical demands
Depersonalization Disconnection from patients as a protective response
Reduced Efficacy Loss of meaning and confidence in clinical contribution
